12

Use AI Agents and the Oracle Analytics AI
Assistant to Interact with Data

                       Learn how to use AI agents and the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant to empower consumers to
                       interact with data conversationally, enabling intuitive data exploration, analysis, and insights
                       discovery through natural language interfaces.

                       Topics:

                       ｷ About Oracle Analytics AI Agents
                       ｷ Create an Oracle Analytics AI Agent
                       ｷ Make an Oracle Analytics AI Agent Available to a Workbook
                       ｷ Modify an Oracle Analytics AI Agent
                       ｷ Delete an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

About Oracle Analytics AI Agents

                       Oracle Analytics AI Agents enable you to define custom prompt instructions and incorporate
                       organizational knowledge into Oracle Analytics AI Assistant interactions.

                       AI agents allow the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant to interpret natural-language questions with
                       greater accuracy and deliver more meaningful, context-aware insights. Oracle Analytics AI
                       Agents use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to enhance generative AI with enterprise
                       data, enabling large language models (LLMs) to "look up" relevant information before
                       responding.

AI agents provide reliable, context-rich insights by combining business data with tailored LLM
instructions and private documentation. AI agents can help your teams move beyond static
dashboards to interactive, conversational analytics that accelerate insight discovery and
improve business performance.

When you submit a question to an Oracle Analytics AI Agent:

ｷ The agent automatically applies additional custom instructions created by the author,
     adding important context such as domain-specific definitions or guidance on preferred
     response formats.

ｷ A RAG process scans the selected enterprise documents configured by the author,
     enriching your prompt with focused, relevant information. The enhanced query is then
     processed by the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant, which orchestrates the underlying LLM
     prompts to deliver a response that best reflects your intent and the available enterprise
     data.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                                 Page 1 of 8
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                             Chapter 12
                                                                 About Oracle Analytics AI Agents

                       When you set up an AI agent in Oracle Analytics, you'll configure four primary components:

                       ｷ Dataset ｭ The foundational data source that powers your agent's analytics and responses.
                             It contains the core business data the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant relies on to answer
                             user questions.

                       ｷ Supplemental Instructions ｭ Custom guidance that shapes how the Oracle Analytics AI
                             Assistant interprets user intent and formulates responses. These instructions influence the
                             Oracle Analytics AI Assistant's reasoning and behavior. For example, by defining business
                             terminology, specifying fiscal logic, outlining naming conventions, or clarifying domain
                             rules. In essence, they teach the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant to think and communicate in
                             your organization's language. Everything included here is passed directly to the AI without
                             preprocessing. See About Supplemental Instructions in an Oracle Analytics AI Agent.

                       ｷ Welcome Message ｭ This is the introductory message users see when they first interact
                             with the AI Agent. It can describe the agent's purpose and provide sample questions to
                             help users get started.

                       ｷ Knowledge Documents ｭ A collection of supporting materials such as policies, reports,
                             FAQs, or reference guides. You can upload PDF or .txt files, which are used through
                             Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), enabling the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant to
                             "look up" and cite factual information directly from your content rather than relying solely on
                             its pre-existing training. Knowledge Documents (RAG) define what information from your
                             private knowledge base can assist the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant in answering a
                             specific question. Only the document excerpts that are relevant to the user's query are
                             shared with the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant. Unrelated content isn't included. See About
                             Knowledge Documents Used in an Oracle Analytics AI Agent.

When to Use an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

                       This section outlines appropriate scenarios for using an Oracle Analytics AI Agent, ensuring
                       optimal use of automated insights and efficiency in your analytics workflows.

                       AI agents are domain-specific functional experts. Think of an AI agent as a subject-matter
                       expert in a specific business domain, supporting focused Oracle Analytics AI Assistant
                       interactions. The more focused its scope, the more precise and meaningful its responses will
                       be.

                       AI agents are designed to support Oracle Analytics AI Assistant users within a clearly-defined
                       business domain. Each agent is associated with a specific dataset, curated knowledge
                       documents, and tailored instructions. Together, these components significantly improve the
                       ability of the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant and underlying LLM to interpret user questions
                       accurately within a particular context.

                       By reducing ambiguity and grounding the AI in domain-specific semantics, instructions, and
                       documentation, organizations can create highly specialized AI agents. For example, you can
                       create an AI agent focused on the company's sales backlog, another AI agent with an
                       employee attrition focus, or an AI agent focused on customer service request volumes. Each

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                                 Page 2 of 8
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                                           Chapter 12
                                                                                                                  About Oracle Analytics AI Agents

                       AI agent draws from a targeted set of data sources, applies customized response
                       expectations, and incorporates relevant business knowledge such as competitive information,
                       HR policies, or service level agreements.
                       AI agents are created by authors for use by consumers. Consumers can view dashboards or
                       workbooks and rely on the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant to interact with their data using
                       natural-language questions. Consumers don't create new visualizations themselves, instead
                       they use agents to obtain quick clarifications, follow-up answers, and drill-down insights within
                       the context of the analytics content they are exploring.
                       You can use Oracle Analytics AI Agents within a workbook or independently.
                       ｷ Consumers can use agents that have been enabled in a workbook. See Attach an Oracle

                             Analytics AI Agent to a Workbook.
                       ｷ Authors can use agents in preview mode. See Modify an Oracle Analytics AI Agent.

About the Dataset Used in an AI Agent

                       In Oracle Analytics, you can associate any dataset with an AI agent.
                       Oracle Analytics Cloud allows one dataset per AI agent. You can use a dataset based on a file,
                       a table, or multi-table from a connection, a local subject area, or subject area.
                       When you associate a dataset with an AI agent, you must ensure the dataset is indexed for
                       use with the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant. See Make Data Available for Oracle Analytics AI
                       Assistant for more information on making data available for the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant.

                       In the AI agent editor, you can preview the dataset's metadata, which includes the full list of
                       available columns and the Index Type and Synonyms configured for each column. Use this
                       editor to select a subset of columns and define agent specific filters.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                                 Page 3 of 8
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                             Chapter 12
                                                                 About Oracle Analytics AI Agents

                       Column Subsetting

                       You can activate or deactivate any indexed column to control which fields the agent can
                       reference when answering questions. If a user asks about a column that isn't activated, the
                       Oracle Analytics AI Assistant won't respond using that data. Columns that aren't indexed in the
                       dataset can't be enabled for the agent and won't be available for selection.

                       Agent Filters

                       You can define data filters specific to the agent. These filters are automatically applied to all
                       user questions processed by the agent and can't be overridden by consumers. This ensures
                       that all interactions remain within the intended data scope.

About Supplemental Instructions in an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

                       Supplemental instructions play a crucial role in shaping how an AI agent interprets user
                       questions and behaves within a specific business context.

                       Datasets and knowledge documents provide factual information. Supplemental instructions
                       define the logic, expectations, and interpretation rules that guide the Oracle Analytics AI
                       Assistant's reasoning. They act as the agent's operating manual by reducing ambiguity,
                       minimizing hallucinations, and ensuring that responses align with your organization's
                       terminology, standards, and domain logic rather than generic AI assumptions.

                       Custom supplemental instructions currently support up to 6,000 characters per agent.

                       Oracle recommends using the following structure for your supplemental instructions.

                       1. Intent / Objective
                             Describe when the rule applies or what it is intended to achieve. For example, Apply this
                             rule when a user asks about Important Orders or Regular Orders.

                       2. Definition / Use Case Description
                             Specify how the agent should interpret or classify the concept. For example, Use the
                             following mapping: `Low, Medium, Not Specified' = Regular Orders; `High,
                             Critical' = Important Orders.

                       3. Examples / Rules / Conditions

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                                 Page 4 of 8
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                                           Chapter 12
                                                                                                                  About Oracle Analytics AI Agents

                             List the triggers, keywords, or conditions that determine when the rule should be executed.
                             For example When terms like `important,' `regular,' or `priority comparison'
                             appear, apply the above Order Priority mapping.

                       Using this structure ensures your instructions are unambiguous and makes it easier for the AI
                       agent to understand business intent and deliver accurate, context-aware responses.

About Knowledge Documents Used in an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

                       Knowledge documents allow you to provide internal information such as process
                       documentation, enterprise policies, competitive insights, market data, budgets, objectives, or
                       other domain-specific knowledge.

                       Your content remains private within your Oracle Analytics instance and is used solely to enrich
                       Oracle Analytics AI Assistant prompts so the agent can produce more precise answers. After
                       you upload knowledge documents, they are parsed and vectorized. A query retrieves and adds
                       only the most relevant portions to the prompt, ensuring that responses remain accurate and
                       contextually aligned.

                       You must save the agent once before you can upload knowledge documents. As you upload
                       documents, an indexing process is initiated. Your document is ready for use by the agent once
                       its status on the list shows it as ready.

                       You can use documents written in English and other supported languages. Text-heavy
                       documents with clear punctuation are preferred. Avoid primarily tabular data, which is more
                       difficult to split and vectorize effectively. Watch for conflicting information. If documents contain
                       contradictory facts, for example, one states "return rate objective is 10%," and another says
                       "5%", the RAG process may favor whichever information it retrieves first. To ensure reliable
                       answers, avoid or reconcile conflicting information across your documents.

Oracle Analytics Knowledge Document File Considerations

                       Learn about important file considerations when working with Oracle Analytics knowledge
                       documents, such as supported formats and size limitations.

                       ｷ You need to save the agent once before uploading a knowledge document.

                       ｷ PDF and TXT files are supported.

                       ｷ Images inside documents aren't supported and are ignored.

                       ｷ You can upload up to 10 knowledge document files to each agent. Each file must be under
                             5 MB.

                       ｷ Upload your files as-is. There's no need to reformat your files for use with an agent.

Upload a Knowledge Document to an AI Agent

                       Learn how to upload a knowledge document to an AI agent to enhance its ability to provide
                       accurate and relevant answers.

                       1. On your home page, find the agent you want to add a knowledge document.

                       2. Hover over the agent, click Actions, and then click Open.

                       3. Click Edit the Agent.

                       4. Click Upload new document. Navigate to the PDF or txt file you wish to upload and click
                             Open.

                       5. Enter a title for the document then click Save.

Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                                           Chapter 12
                                                                                                               Create an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

                             Once uploaded, the document will display an indexing status, indicating that the system is
                             processing and preparing the document for use. Wait a few seconds for the indexing
                             process to complete.You can refresh the page or panel to update the document's status.
                             The status will change to Ready once indexing is finished.
                       6. Once your document is indexed and ready, you can interact with it in the following ways:
                             ｷ Edit: Modify the document's title as needed.
                             ｷ Replace: Upload a new document to replace the existing one.
                             ｷ Download: Retrieve a copy of the uploaded document.
                             ｷ Delete: Remove the document from the knowledge base.

Create an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

                       When you create an AI Agent in Oracle Analytics, you bring together a dataset, a set of custom
                       instructions to be passed to the Oracle Analytics AI Assistant, and specific knowledge
                       documentation.

                       Ensure that your administrator has managed permissions related to AI agent use and
                       configuration. See: Enable Users to Create AI Agents in Oracle Analytics.
                       1. On your Home Page, click Create then click AI Agent.

2. Select a dataset then click Add to Agent.
3. Configure the agent on the New AI Agent screen. Enter supplemental instructions, then

     click Save.
4. Enter a name for the agent, and optionally enter a description, then click OK.
5. Upload one or more knowledge documents.
6. Click Save.

                       7. Click Preview to run the agent. Enter test prompts and verify that the AI agent returns
                             expected results.

                       You can share the AI agent with a specific set of users. See Control Access to Oracle Analytics
                       AI Agents.

Make an Oracle Analytics AI Agent Available to a Workbook

                       When you enable an AI agent in a workbook, users with access to the workbook can access
                       and interact with the AI agent. For example, users might use an AI agent within the context of a
                       workbook to obtain quick clarifications, follow-up answers, and drill-down insights within the
                       context of the analytics content they're exploring.

                       Topics:

                       ｷ Attach an Oracle Analytics AI Agent to a Workbook
                       ｷ Enable an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

Attach an Oracle Analytics AI Agent to a Workbook

                       Learn how to associate an Oracle Analytics AI Agent with a workbook to enable automated
                       insights and enhanced data interaction.

                       1. On your home page, hover over the workbook where you want to enable the agent,
                             Actions, and then click Open.

                       2. Click the Visualize tab, then click Auto Insights .

                       3. Click the Assistant tab, then AI Agents .
                       4. Click the name of the agent, click OK, then Save.

Enable an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

                       To make an AI agent available to consumers, the author must enable the Workbook Assistant
                       through the Present Mode configuration.

                       Once an author enables the Workbook Assistant, the attached agent is enabled by default. For
                       details, see Specify Workbook Assistant Data Source.

                              Note
                                 Oracle Analytics AI Agents are available to end users only when dashboards are
                                 accessed in Present Mode. This configuration is intended for dashboard consumers,
                                 enabling authors to design dashboards and attach Agents specifically for the end-user
                                 experience. Authors will continue to leverage the dataset associated with the
                                 workbook while interacting with Oracle Analytics AI Assistant and not the AI agent.

Modify an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

                       You can update and extend an AI agent at any time. For example, you might modify the agent
                       dataset definition, edit the supplementary instructions, fine-tune the start up message, add or
                       update knowledge documents, and so on.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                                 Page 7 of 8

                                                                                                                                                           Chapter 12
                                                                                                               Delete an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

                       1. On your home page, hover over the agent you want to edit, click Actions, and then click
                             Open.

                       2. Click Edit the Agent.
                       3. Modify the details of the agent as needed, then click Save.

                       4. Click Preview to run the agent. Enter test prompts and verify that the AI agent returns
                             expected results.

                       5. If needed, make further edits, preview the changes, and test the AI agent again.

Delete an Oracle Analytics AI Agent

                       Learn how to permanently delete an AI agent in Oracle Analytics, ensuring that related data
                       and schedules are no longer active.
                       1. On your home page, find the agent you want to edit.
                       2. Hover over the agent, click Actions, and then click Delete.

Filter Data

                       Use filters in Oracle Analytics to focus the data in your workbooks and create an effective and
                       interactive experience for your consumers.

                       Topics:
                       ｷ About Filters
                       ｷ How Do Filters Limit Each Other?
                       ｷ Filter Properties
                       ｷ Filter Personalizations and Workbook States
                       ｷ Use the Filter Bar
                       ｷ Dashboard Filters
                       ｷ Visualization Filters
                       ｷ Filter Groups (Preview)
                       ｷ Selection Steps
                       ｷ Change the Filter Type
                       ｷ Top Bottom N Filters
                       ｷ Create an Expression Filter
                       ｷ Exclude Selected Filter Values
                       ｷ Include Null Filter Values
                       ｷ Disable Multi Select for Filters
                       ｷ Disable a Filter or Selection Step

About Filters

                       Filters reduce the amount of data shown in visualizations. Oracle Analytics lets you filter the
                       data in your workbooks in many different ways and configure the filter properties to customize
                       the experience for your consumers.

                       Topics:
                       ｷ Filter Scopes
                       ｷ Filter Types
                       ｷ How the Number of Datasets Affects Filters
                       ｷ Ways to Use Parameters with Filters

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                             Page 1 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                    Chapter 13
                                                                 About Filters

Filter Scopes

                       You can apply filters at different scopes in your workbook in Oracle Analytics, from broadly
                       filtering the entire workbook down to filtering a single visualization on a canvas.

                       You can apply filters at the following scopes:

                       ｷ Workbook - A workbook filter is pinned in the filter bar and applies to all canvases in the
                             workbook. See Use the Filter Bar.

                       ｷ Canvas - There are two types of filters you can use for a canvas:

                             ｭ A canvas filter is unpinned in the filter bar and applies only to a single canvas in the
                                  workbook. See Use the Filter Bar.

                             ｭ A dashboard filter is used directly on a canvas to enable consumers to interact with
                                  and filter data on the canvas. See Dashboard Filters.

                       ｷ Visualization - A visualization filter is used directly on a visualization and applies only to
                             that visualization. See Visualization Filters.

Filter Types

                       Oracle Analytics supports many different types of filters that you can use depending on the
                       filter scope and the data element you're using to filter.

Filter Type  Description
List
             A list filter can be applied to data elements that are text and date data types, and
             number data types that can't be aggregated. A list filter provides a simple list of values
             to choose from and lets you see which values are selected and which aren't. They
             work well for attributes with a long list of values.

             Availability: List filters are available for all filter scopes.

             Properties:
             ｷ Multi Select
             ｷ Default Value
             ｷ Exclude
             ｷ Nulls
             ｷ Disable
             ｷ Selection Parameter Binding
             ｷ Excludes Mode Parameter Binding
             For more information about these properties, see Dashboard Filter Control Properties.

             Limitations: While list filters don't have a limit to the number of values they can
             display, performance can decline if more than 10,000 values are selected.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                             Page 2 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                    Chapter 13
                                                                 About Filters

Filter Type   Description
List box
              A list box filter is similar to a list filter and can be applied to text, uncountable values,
Checkbox /    and dates. It also provides a simple list of values but only lets you select one or All.
Radio Button
              Availability: List box filters are available only for dashboard filters.
Top Bottom N
              Properties:
              ｷ Custom Values
              ｷ Default Value
              ｷ Exclude
              ｷ Nulls
              ｷ Selection Parameter Binding
              ｷ Excludes Mode Parameter Binding
              For more information about these properties, see Dashboard Filter Control Properties.

              Limitations:
              ｷ You can't disable a list box filter.
              ｷ Multi Select isn't available for list box filters.

              A checkbox or radio button filter can be applied to text, uncountable values, and dates.
              When Multi Select is on, the filter uses a checkbox, and when it's off, the filter uses a
              radio button.

              Availability: Checkbox or radio button filters are available only for dashboard filters.

              Properties:
              ｷ Multi Select
              ｷ Default Value
              ｷ Exclude
              ｷ Nulls
              ｷ Visible Values
              ｷ Selection Parameter Binding
              ｷ Excludes Mode Parameter Binding
              For more information about these properties, see Dashboard Filter Control Properties.

              Limitations:
              ｷ By default, this filter type is optimized to display only the first 50 data values. For a

                    filter column with more than 50 values, Oracle recommends that you use a
                    different filter type, for example a list box filter.
              ｷ You can't disable a checkbox or radio button filter.

              A top bottom N filter can be applied to measure and attribute data elements. For
              example, you might filter a visualization by the top 10 customers based on sales.

              Availability: Top bottom N filters are available for all filter scopes. For more
              information on how to use top bottom N filters, see Top Bottom N Filters.

              Properties:
              ｷ Method - Specifies whether to filter by Top (highest values) or Bottom (lowest

                    values).
              ｷ Count - Specifies the number of values to display.
              ｷ By - Specifies which measure or attribute to limit by.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                             Page 3 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                    Chapter 13
                                                                 About Filters

Filter Type    Description
Range
               A range filter can be generated for data elements that are number data types and that
Date Range     have an aggregation rule set to something other than none. Range filters are applied
               to data elements that are measures, and that limit data to a range of contiguous
Relative Time  values, such as revenue of $100,000 to $500,000. Or you can create a range filter that
               excludes (as opposed to includes) a contiguous range of values. Such exclusive filters
               limit data to noncontiguous ranges (for example, revenue less than $100,000 or
               greater than $500,000).

               Availability: Range filters are available for all filter scopes.

               Properties:

               ｷ Start - Specifies the minimum value.
               ｷ End - Specifies the maximum value.
               ｷ By - Specifies which measure or attribute to limit by.

               A date range filter uses calendar controls to adjust time or date selections to limit data
               to a specific period of time. You can either select a single contiguous range of dates, or
               you can use a date range filter to exclude dates within the specified range.

               Availability: Date range filters are available for all filter scopes.

               Properties:

               ｷ Range - Requires a start and end date to filter on.
               ｷ Start at - Requires only a start date to filter from.
               ｷ End at - Requires only an end date to filter to.
               ｷ Equal - Requires a specific date to filter on.

               A relative time filter focuses on values for a specified time period relative to today or
               end of last period. For example, you can focus on the last three years, the next three
               years, or year to date through the last fiscal period.

               The current date and time used in queries is the Oracle Analytics server host's date
               and time in the server's timezone (not the browser host's time or timezone).

               Availability: Relative time filters are available for all filter scopes.

               Properties:
               ｷ Type - Specifies whether the filter is relative to a past or future time period, or to

                     today's date.
                     ｭ Last - Uses the Increment and Time Level values to specify a time period up

                           to either today's date or the end of the last period.
                           Last filters that are based on a DateTime column and which have a grain of
                           Day or longer (for example, Year, Quarter, Month, Week, Day), retrieve data
                           from the same time of day on the starting day. For example, if the server date/
                           time is currently Thursday 3:15pm , a Last 2 Days filter on a DateTime
                           column retrieves data with timestamps between Tuesday 3:15pm and
                           Thursday 3:15pm in the server's timezone. Filter queries that are based on a
                           Date column (which by definition have no time of day associated) only
                           depend on the server host's date, not the time of day.
                     ｭ Next - Uses the Increment and Time Level values to specify a time period
                           after either today's date or the start of the next period.
                     ｭ To Date - Use the Time Level value to specify the time period from the
                           beginning of the Time Level value up to today's date. For example, Month to
                           Date retrieves data from midnight of the first day of this month up until the
                           current date and time (that is, Today).
               ｷ Increment - Specifies the increment of the unit of time to use for the filter.
               ｷ Time Level - Specifies the unit of time to use for the filter.
               ｷ Relative To - Specifies whether the filtered data is relative to Today, Start of Next
                     Period, or End of Last Period.

               Limitations: You can apply a relative time filter only to date columns that already exist
               in the data source, not to derived columns such as Year, or Quarter. The relative time
               filter type supports Date and DateTime column types.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                             Page 4 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                    Chapter 13
                                                                 About Filters

Filter Type  Description
Slider
             A slider filter can be used to animate visualizations and show dynamically how your
             data changes over a given dimension such as time. A slider filter can be applied to
             text, uncountable values, and dates.
             Availability: Slider filters are only available for dashboard filters. See Filter and
             Animate Visualizations Using a Slider Dashboard Filter.

             Properties:
             ｷ Font - Value and Label
             ｷ Auto Play - On, Off, Speed, and Repeat
             For more information about these properties, see Dashboard Filter Control Properties.

             Limitations:
             ｷ You can't disable a slider filter.
             ｷ Parameter binding isn't available for slider filters.

To change the filter type of an existing filter, see Change the Filter Type.

The following filter options are also available in Oracle Analytics:

ｷ Expression Filter - An expression filter lets you define more complex filters using SQL
     expressions. See Create an Expression Filter.

ｷ Use as Filter - A visualization can be used to filter other visualizations on the same
     canvas. See Use a Visualization as a Filter.

ｷ Selection Steps - Selection steps let you filter hierarchical columns in visualizations to
     refine the data displayed. See Selection Steps.

How the Number of Datasets Affects Filters

Filters can interact differently with visualizations depending on the number of datasets and
whether the datasets are joined.

If there's a single dataset in a workbook, and you add a filter, there are no restrictions on which
data the filter applies to.

If a workbook contains multiple datasets, make sure that they're joined if you want to use data
elements from one dataset as filters in your workbook. Filters based on data elements from
one dataset don't work on visualizations using data from another dataset if the datasets aren't
joined.

Ways to Use Parameters with Filters

In Oracle Analytics, you can use parameters and filters together to dynamically manipulate the
data in your workbook.

Use parameters and filters together in the following ways:

ｷ Bind a parameter to a filter to pass a selected filter value to the parameter everywhere it's
     used in the workbook. See Bind Parameters to Filters.

ｷ Add a parameter to the filter bar to set the parameter value for the workbook or canvas.
     See Use a Parameter in the Filter Bar.

ｷ Add a parameter to a dashboard filter visualization as a filter control. See Use a Parameter
     as a Dashboard Filter Control.

If you use a parameter as a filter, keep in mind that parameters have a limit of 10,000 available
values that can be cached and also a limit of 1,000 initial values.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                             Page 5 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                                           Chapter 13
                                                                                                                  How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

                       You can also use a parameter and a filter together to conditionally show or hide visualizations
                       on a canvas based on a user's selections. See Use a Parameter to Conditionally Show or Hide
                       a Visualization.

How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

                       The topics in this section explain how different filters limit each other in workbooks in Oracle
                       Analytics.

                       Topics:

                       ｷ About the Limit Values Settings for Filters
                       ｷ Specify Limit Values By for the Filter Bar
                       ｷ Specify Limit Values for Workbook and Canvas Filters
                       ｷ Specify Limit Values By for Dashboard Filters
                       ｷ Specify Limit Values for Visualization Filters
                       ｷ Specify Which Filters to Apply to a Visualization
                       ｷ Apply a Dashboard Filter to Visualizations on the Canvas
                       ｷ Example ｭ Change the Limit Values Setting for a Canvas Filter
                       ｷ Example ｭ Change the Limit Values Setting for a Dashboard Filter
                       ｷ Example ｭ Change the Limit Values Setting for a Visualization Filter

About the Limit Values Settings for Filters

                       In Oracle Analytics, the selections you make for one filter can impact the values available to
                       select from for other filters on your canvas. The impact depends on the filter's location and the
                       Limit Values settings.

                       The Limit Values settings in a workbook operate based on filter location rather than filter scope.
                       The filter location is where the filter is added in the workbook, whereas the filter scope is the
                       range of data the filter acts on. Filters in higher locations normally limit filters in lower locations.

                       The filter locations in a workbook, listed from highest to lowest, are:
                       1. Filter bar
                       2. Dashboard filters visualizations
                       3. Individual visualizations (for visualization filters)

                       For information about filter scopes, see Filter Scopes.

                       For example, you can filter a visualization that shows Sales by Customer Segment using filters
                       in the three different filter locations. You add a filter in the filter bar using the column Product
                       Category, a dashboard filter on your canvas with the column Product Sub Category as the filter
                       control, and a visualization filter on the visualization itself using the column Product Container.

                       If you select Furniture in your Product Category filter, the values available to select from for
                       Product Sub Category are limited to only those product subcategories that fall in the Furniture
                       product category.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                             Page 6 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                 Chapter 13
                                                                                          How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

The values available to select from for Product Container are also limited to only those that fall
in the Furniture product category.
If you then select Tables for Product Sub Category, the values for Product Container are further
limited to only those used for that product subcategory.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                             Page 7 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                             Chapter 13
                                                                 How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

Default Filter Behavior

The default behavior in a workbook for filters from the same dataset or subject area is as
follows:

ｷ The selections you make for a filter limit the values of any other filters in lower locations.

ｷ The selections you make for a filter also limit the values of any other filters in the same
     location, regardless of filter scope.
     In the filter bar, this means that selections for canvas filters can limit workbook filter values.
     In a dashboard filter, the selections for one dashboard filter control can limit any other
     dashboard filter controls. If there's more than one visualization filter on a visualization, the
     selections for one can limit the others.

ｷ If you add a visualization filter on a dashboard filters visualization, that filter limits the
     values for the dashboard filter controls.

Note

Administrators can change this default behavior by configuring the system setting
Default Limit Values By for Filters. See Default Limit Values By for Filter. Ask your
administrator what this system setting is set to before you configure any limit values
settings within your workbook.

                       Limit Values Settings

                       As an author, you can change the default filter behavior on your canvas by configuring the
                       following settings:

                       ｷ The Limit Values By property in the Filter Bar Menu applies to all items in the filter bar.
                             See Specify Limit Values By for the Filter Bar.

                       ｷ The Limit Values setting in the Filter dialog for individual workbook, canvas, and
                             visualization filters applies to only that filter. See Specify Limit Values for Workbook and
                             Canvas Filters and Specify Limit Values for Visualization Filters.

                       ｷ The Limit Values By property for dashboard filters applies to only the selected dashboard
                             filter control. See Specify Limit Values By for Dashboard Filters.

                       ｷ The Filter This Viz By property for an individual visualization, including dashboard filters
                             visualizations, lets you prevent filters from a higher location from affecting the filters in the
                             visualization. See Specify Which Filters to Apply to a Visualization.

                       Configuring Limit Values settings isn't supported for Top Bottom N filters.

Specify Limit Values By for the Filter Bar

                       Configure the Limit Values By setting for the filter bar to specify how any workbook filters,
                       canvas filters, and selection steps in the filter bar get their selection values.

                       This setting doesn't apply to parameters in the filter bar.

                       The options for the Limit Values By setting in the Filter Bar Menu are:

                       ｷ Default ｭ Uses the system setting configured by your administrator.

                       ｷ Auto ｭ Limits the filters in the filter bar by the other filters in the filter bar, based on drill
                             path and regardless of scope.

                       ｷ None ｭ Prevents all filters in the filter bar from limiting each other.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                             Page 8 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                             Chapter 13
                                                                 How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

1. On your home page, hover over a workbook, click Actions, then select Open.
2. Click Filter Bar Menu, then hover over Limit Values By .
3. Click an option to select it.
4. Click Save.

Specify Limit Values for Workbook and Canvas Filters

Configure the Limit Values setting for individual workbook filters and canvas filters in the filter
bar to specify how the filter gets its selection values.

The options for this Limit Values setting are:

ｷ Default - For a workbook (pinned) or canvas (unpinned) filter, uses the Limit Values By
     setting from the Filter Bar Menu.

ｷ Auto ｭ Limits the filter's values by the other filters in the filter bar, based on drill path and
     regardless of scope.

ｷ None ｭ Prevents all other filters in the filter bar from limiting the filter's values.

ｷ Custom ｭ Lets you choose which filters from the filter bar you want to limit the filter's
     values by. For a workbook filter, only other workbook filters are available to choose from. It
     isn't possible to limit a workbook filter by a specific canvas filter because other canvases in
     the workbook may not use that canvas filter.

To specify the limit values for selection steps, see Specify Limit Values for Selection Steps. To
specify the limit values for filter groups, see Specify Limit Values for Filter Groups (Preview).

1. On your home page, hover over a workbook, click Actions, then select Open.
2. Click the filter to open the Filter dialog.

3. Click Limit Values  and click an option to select it.
4. Click Save.

Specify Limit Values By for Dashboard Filters

Configure the Limit Values By setting for individual dashboard filter controls in a dashboard
filters visualization to specify how the filter gets its selection values.

The options for this Limit Values By setting are:

ｷ Default - Limits the filter based on the system setting configured by your administrator.

ｷ Auto ｭ Limits the filter by all filters in the filter bar. Also limits the filter by other filter
     controls in the same dashboard filters visualization or in other dashboard filters
     visualizations on the canvas, providing the other filter controls are on a higher drill path or
     unrelated by drill path.

ｷ None ｭ Prevents all other filter controls in the dashboard filters visualization from limiting
     the filter's values. The filter is still limited by filters in the filter bar.

ｷ Custom ｭ Lets you choose which other dashboard filter controls you want to limit the
     filter's values by. If there's more than one dashboard filters visualization on the canvas,
     dashboard filter controls from all are available to choose from. The filter is still limited by
     filters in the filter bar.

To specify the limit values for selection steps, see Specify Limit Values for Selection Steps.

1. On your home page, hover over a workbook, click Actions, then select Open.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                             Page 9 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                 Chapter 13
                                                                                          How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

2. Click the dashboard filters visualization to select it.
3. Click Properties, then click Filter Controls.
4. Expand the filter you want to set the Limit Values By for.
5. In the Limit Values By row, click Default and select how you want to limit the filter's

     selection values.

                       6. Click Save.

Specify Limit Values for Visualization Filters

                       Configure the Limit Values setting for individual visualization filters to specify how a filter gets
                       its selection values.

                       The options for this Limit Values setting are:

                       ｷ Default - Limits the filter based on the system setting configured by your administrator.

                       ｷ Auto ｭ Limits the filter by all filters in higher locations. Also limits the filter by any other
                             visualization filters on the visualization, providing they're on a higher drill path or unrelated
                             by drill path.

                       ｷ None ｭ Prevents all other visualization filters on the visualization from limiting the filter's
                             values. The filter is still limited by filters in higher locations.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                           Page 10 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                             Chapter 13
                                                                 How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

ｷ Custom ｭ Lets you choose which other visualization filters on the visualization you want to
     limit the filter's values by. The filter is still limited by filters in higher locations.

To specify the limit values for selection steps, see Specify Limit Values for Selection Steps. To
specify the limit values for filter groups, see Specify Limit Values for Filter Groups (Preview).

1. On your home page, hover over a workbook, click Actions, then select Open.
2. Click the filter on the visualization to open the Filter dialog.

3. Click Limit Values  and click an option to select it.
4. Click Save.

Specify Which Filters to Apply to a Visualization

When you add a visualization to a workbook, Oracle Analytics applies all the existing
workbook, canvas, and dashboard filters to the visualization. You can use the Filter This Viz
By property to specify which filters you don't want to apply to a visualization.

This property applies to dashboard filter visualizations too.

You can use this property when you want to stop a filter in a higher location from limiting a
dashboard filter or a visualization filter.

Any selection steps or filter groups (preview) that affect the visualization are also available to
choose from using this property.

1. On your home page, hover over a workbook, click Actions, then select Open.

2. Click a visualization to select it.

3. Click Properties to open the Properties pane, then click Filters.

4. In the Filter This Viz By section, deselect the items you don't want to apply to the
     visualization.

                       5. Click Save.                            February 11, 2026
                                                                     Page 11 of 101
Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud
G39820-07
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                                           Chapter 13
                                                                                                                  How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

Apply a Dashboard Filter to Visualizations on the Canvas

                       When you add a dashboard filter, Oracle Analytics applies it to all visualizations on the canvas.
                       You can specify which of the visualizations you don't want to apply the dashboard filter to.
                       1. On your home page, hover over a workbook, click Actions, then select Open.
                       2. Click the dashboard filters visualization to select it.
                       3. Click Properties to open the Properties pane, then click Filters.
                       4. In the Apply This Filter To section, deselect the visualizations that you don't want the filter

                             to apply to.

                       5. Click Save.

Example ｭ Change the Limit Values Setting for a Canvas Filter

                       This example shows how to configure the Limit Values setting for a canvas (unpinned) filter in
                       the filter bar.
                       In this example, you have a bar chart showing Sales by Product Sub Category.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                           Page 12 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                             Chapter 13
                                                                 How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

You add three filters to the filter bar:

1. In the Data pane, expand Ship Date and drag Ship Date (Year) to the filter bar.

2. In the filter bar, hover over Ship Date (Year) and click Pin to All Canvases to make it a
     workbook filter.

3. In the Data pane, drag Ship Date (Quarter) to the filter bar.

4. In the Data pane, drag Ship Mode to the filter bar.

The Limit Values By setting for the filter bar is set to Default. The Limit Values settings for
each of these three filters are also set to Default.

Next, you change the Limit Values for Ship Date (Quarter) from Default to Auto, None, and
Custom to see how that canvas filter is affected each time.

Here's what happens if you change the Limit Values setting for Ship Date (Quarter) to Auto:

The values available to select from for Ship Date (Quarter) are limited by the selections you
make for the workbook filter Ship Date (Year) and by the selections you make for the other
canvas filter Ship Mode.

1. Select 2023 for Ship Date (Year). The values available to select for Ship Date (Quarter) are
     limited to the quarters in 2023.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                           Page 13 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                             Chapter 13
                                                                 How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

2. Then select Regular Air for Ship Mode. The values available to select for Ship Date
     (Quarter) are now limited to the first three quarters of 2023 because there were no
     shipments using Regular Air in Q4.

Here's what happens if you change the Limit Values setting for Ship Date (Quarter) to None:

ｷ All the quarters in the dataset are available to select from for Ship Date (Quarter),
     regardless of the selections you made for Ship Date (Year) or for Ship Mode.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                           Page 14 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                 Chapter 13
                                                                                          How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

ｷ All three of the filters in the filter bar apply to the visualization regardless of the Limit
     Values settings. This means that if you select Q4 2021 for Ship Date (Quarter), you get a
     query error and no data displays in the visualization because it's still limited to the first
     three quarters of 2023.

Here's what happens if you change the Limit Values setting for Ship Date (Quarter) to a
custom option by selecting Ship Date (Year), which is the workbook filter:
ｷ The values available to select for Ship Date (Quarter) are limited by the selection you

     made for Ship Date (Year) but not by the selection you made for Ship Mode. All four
     quarters of 2023 are available because the Ship Mode filter selection of Regular Air is
     ignored.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                           Page 15 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                                           Chapter 13
                                                                                                                  How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

                       ｷ In this scenario, while the canvas filter Ship Date (Quarter) is only limited by Ship Date
                             (Year), all three filters still apply to the visualization itself. This means that if you select Q4
                             2023 for Ship Date (Quarter), no data displays in the visualization because there were no
                             shipments using the ship mode Regular Air in that quarter.

Example ｭ Change the Limit Values Setting for a Dashboard Filter

                       This example shows how to configure the Limit Values setting for a dashboard filter.

                       In this example, you have a bar chart showing Sales by Product Sub Category.

You create three filters:

1. In the Data pane, drag City to the filter bar.

2. Click Visualizations and drag Dashboard Filters to your canvas.

3. Click Data and drag Product Category and Product Container to the dashboard filters
     visualization.

The Limit Values By setting for the filter bar is set to Default. The Limit Values settings for
each of these three filters are also set to Default.

Next, you change the Limit Values for Product Container from Default to Auto, None, and
Custom to see how that dashboard filter is affected each time.

Here's what happens if you change the Limit Values setting for Product Container to Auto:

The values available to select from for Product Container are limited by the selections you
make for the canvas filter City and by the selections you make for the other dashboard filter
control Product Category.

1. Select Austin for City. There are only six values available to select for Product Container
     because the container Medium Box isn't used for that city.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                           Page 16 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                             Chapter 13
                                                                 How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

2. Then select Office Supplies for Product Category. The values available to select for
     Product Container are further reduced to just four, those that are used for office supplies in
     Austin.

Here's what happens if you change the Limit Values setting for Product Container to None:

ｷ The values available to select from for Product Container are only limited by the selection
     you made for the canvas filter City. All six of the Product Containers associated with Austin

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                           Page 17 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                           Chapter 13
                                                                                     How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

are now available. Even though you have Office Supplies selected for Product Category,
that selection has no impact on the Product Container filter.

ｷ In this scenario, while the Product Container filter is limited only by the City filter (because
     it's in a higher filter location), all three filters still apply to the visualization itself. This means
     that if you select Jumbo Box, no data displays in the visualization because that product
     container isn't used for office supplies in Austin.

Here's what happens if you change the Limit Values setting for Product Container to Custom:

ｷ This option is most useful if you have more than two filter controls in your dashboard filter,
     and you want to choose exactly which ones to limit Product Container by.

ｷ If you add a third filter control using the column Product Sub Category, you can configure
     the Limit Values setting for Product Container to be limited by only Product Category or by
     only Product Sub Category, instead of both (Default and Auto) or neither (None).

G39820-07                                                           Page 18 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                             Chapter 13
                                                                 How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

ｷ Product Container is still limited by City.

If you want to stop the City filter from limiting the dashboard filters, you need to go to the Filters
tab in the Properties pane for the dashboard filters visualization. Then, under Filter This Viz
By, deselect City (Canvas).

Example ｭ Change the Limit Values Setting for a Visualization Filter

                       This example shows how to configure the Limit Values setting for a visualization filter.
                       In this example, you have a bar chart showing Sales by Product Sub Category.

Visualizing Data and Building Reports in Oracle Analytics Cloud  February 11, 2026
G39820-07                                                           Page 19 of 101
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.

                                                                 How Do Filters Limit Each Other?

You create four filters:

1. In the Data pane, drag City to the filter bar.

2. Click Visualizations and drag Dashboard Filters to your canvas.

3. Click Data and drag Ship Date (Year) to the dashboard filters visualization.

4. Click the bar chart visualization to select it.

5. In the Data pane, drag Product Sub Category and Product Container to Filters in the
     Grammar pane.

The Limit Values By setting for the filter bar is set to Default. The Limit Values settings for
each of these four filters are also set to Default.

Next, you change the Limit Values for Product Sub Category from Default to Auto, None, and
Custom to see how that visualization filter is affected each time.

Here's what happens if you change the Limit Values setting for Product Sub Category to
Auto:

The values available to select from for Product Sub Category are limited by the selections you
make for the canvas filter City, the dashboard filter Ship Date (Year), and the other visualization
filter Product Container.

1. Select Austin, Dallas, and Houston for City. There are only 15 values available to select
     for Product Sub Category because no products for the sub categories "Copiers and Fax"
     and "Scissors, Rulers and Trimmers" were sold in those three cities.

2. Then select 2023 for Ship Date (Year). The values available to select for Product Sub
     Category are further reduced to 13 to reflect the ones used for the three cities in 2023.

     considered as they're in higher filter locations. The selection you made for Product
     Container, Wrap Bag, is ignored.

                       You can customize the appearance and functionality of filters in Oracle Analytics by setting the

                       settings.

                       ｷ Dashboard Filter General Properties

                       ｷ Dashboard Filter Control Properties
                       ｷ Dashboard and Visualization Filters Properties
                       See also Filter Bar Properties.
